content:
# Numbers Chapter 26 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 179–181 | **Source Image:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/NUM_026_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_026_page_002.jpg`, `NUM_026_page_003.jpg`
**Secondary Source:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pa_0132–0134

---

**1** After the plague had ended, YHWH spoke to Moses and to Eleazar son of Aaron the priest, saying:

**2** "Take a census of the entire community of the Sons of Israel — everyone twenty years old or more, by their ancestral houses — all who are fit for military service in Israel."

**3** So Moses and Eleazar the priest addressed them on the plains of Moab, by the Jordan opposite Jericho, saying:

**4** "Count everyone twenty years old or more, just as YHWH commanded Moses." These were the Sons of Israel who came out of the land of Egypt:

**5** Reuben was Israel's firstborn. The descendants of Reuben: from Hanoch, the Hanochite clan; from Pallu, the Palluite clan;

**6** from Hezron, the Hezronite clan; from Carmi, the Carmite clan.

**7** These were the clans of the Reubenites. Their registered number was forty-three thousand seven hundred and thirty.

**8** And the son of Pallu was Eliab.

**9** And the sons of Eliab were Nemuel, Dathan, and Abiram. These are the same Dathan and Abiram — chosen representatives of the community — who rebelled against Moses and Aaron as part of Korah's faction, when they defied YHWH.

**10** And the earth opened its mouth and swallowed them along with Korah, when that faction perished — when the fire consumed two hundred and fifty men, and they became a warning sign.

**11** But the sons of Korah did not die.

**12** The descendants of Simeon by their clans: from Nemuel, the Nemuelite clan; from Jamin, the Jaminite clan; from Jachin, the Jachinite clan;

**13** from Zerah, the Zerahite clan; from Shaul, the Shaulite clan.

**14** These were the clans of the Simeonites: twenty-two thousand two hundred.

**15** The descendants of Gad by their clans: from Zephon, the Zephonite clan; from Haggi, the Haggite clan; from Shuni, the Shunite clan;

**16** from Ozni, the Oznite clan; from Eri, the Erite clan;

**17** from Arod, the Arodite clan; from Areli, the Arelite clan.

**18** These were the clans of the descendants of Gad. Their registered number was forty thousand five hundred.

**19** The sons of Judah included Er and Onan, but Er and Onan died in the land of Canaan.

**20** And the descendants of Judah by their clans were: from Shelah, the Shelanite clan; from Perez, the Perezite clan; from Zerah, the Zerahite clan.

**21** And the descendants of Perez were: from Hezron, the Hezronite clan; from Hamul, the Hamulite clan.

**22** These were the clans of Judah. Their registered number was seventy-six thousand five hundred.

**23** The descendants of Issachar by their clans: from Tola, the Tolaite clan; from Puvah, the Punite clan;

**24** from Jashub, the Jashubite clan; from Shimron, the Shimronite clan.

**25** These were the clans of Issachar. Their registered number was sixty-four thousand three hundred.

**26** The descendants of Zebulun by their clans: from Sered, the Seredite clan; from Elon, the Elonite clan; from Jahleel, the Jahleelite clan.

**27** These were the clans of the Zebulunites. Their registered number was sixty thousand five hundred.

**28** The descendants of Joseph by their clans: Manasseh and Ephraim.

**29** The descendants of Manasseh: from Machir, the Machirite clan. And Machir fathered Gilead; from Gilead, the Gileadite clan.

**30** These were the descendants of Gilead: from Iezer, the Iezerite clan; from Helek, the Helekite clan;

**31** from Asriel, the Asrielite clan; from Shechem, the Shechemite clan;

**32** from Shemida, the Shemidaite clan; from Hepher, the Hepherite clan.

**33** Now Zelophehad son of Hepher had no sons — only daughters. And the names of Zelophehad's daughters were Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah.

**34** These were the clans of Manasseh. Their registered number was fifty-two thousand seven hundred.

**35** These were the descendants of Ephraim by their clans: from Shuthelah, the Shuthelahite clan; from Becher, the Becherite clan; from Tahan, the Tahanite clan.

**36** And these were the descendants of Shuthelah: from Eran, the Eranite clan.

**37** These were the clans of the descendants of Ephraim. Their registered number was thirty-two thousand five hundred. These were the descendants of Joseph by their clans.

**38** The descendants of Benjamin by their clans: from Bela, the Belaite clan; from Ashbel, the Ashbelite clan; from Ahiram, the Ahiramite clan;

**39** from Shephupham, the Shuphamite clan; from Hupham, the Huphamite clan.

**40** And the sons of Bela were Ard and Naaman: from Ard, the Ardite clan; from Naaman, the Naamite clan.

**41** These were the descendants of Benjamin by their clans. Their registered number was forty-five thousand six hundred.

**42** These were the descendants of Dan by their clans: from Shuham, the Shuhamite clan. These were the clans of Dan by their clans.

**43** All the clans of the Shuhamites — their registered number was sixty-four thousand four hundred.

**44** The descendants of Asher by their clans: from Imnah, the Imnite clan; from Ishvi, the Ishvite clan; from Beriah, the Beriite clan.

**45** From the descendants of Beriah: from Heber, the Heberite clan; from Malchiel, the Malchielite clan.

**46** And the name of Asher's daughter was Serah.

**47** These were the clans of the descendants of Asher. Their registered number was fifty-three thousand four hundred.

**48** The descendants of Naphtali by their clans: from Jahzeel, the Jahzeelite clan; from Guni, the Gunite clan;

**49** from Jezer, the Jezerite clan; from Shillem, the Shillemite clan.

**50** These were the clans of Naphtali by their clans. Their registered number was forty-five thousand four hundred.

**51** The total number of the Sons of Israel who were registered was six hundred and one thousand seven hundred and thirty.

**52** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying:

**53** "The land is to be divided among these tribes as an inheritance, apportioned according to the number of registered names.

**54** To a larger tribe you are to give a larger inheritance, and to a smaller tribe a smaller inheritance. Each tribe is to receive its inheritance in proportion to its registered number.

**55** The land, however, must be divided by lot. They are to receive their inheritance according to the names of their ancestral tribes.

**56** Each inheritance is to be apportioned by lot, whether the tribe is large or small."

**57** These were the Levites who were registered by their clans: from Gershon, the Gershonite clan; from Kohath, the Kohathite clan; from Merari, the Merarite clan.

**58** These were the clans of Levi: the Libnite clan, the Hebronite clan, the Mahlite clan, the Mushite clan, and the Korahite clan. And Kohath fathered Amram.

**59** And the name of Amram's wife was Jochebed daughter of Levi, who was born to Levi in Egypt. And she bore to Amram: Aaron, Moses, and their sister Miriam.

**60** And to Aaron were born Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.

**61** But Nadab and Abihu died when they offered unauthorized fire before YHWH.

**62** Their registered number was twenty-three thousand — every male one month old or more. They were not counted among the Sons of Israel because no inheritance was given to them among the Sons of Israel.

**63** These were the ones registered by Moses and Eleazar the priest, who counted the Sons of Israel on the plains of Moab, by the Jordan opposite Jericho.

**64** And among these there was not a single person who had been counted in the census taken by Moses and Aaron the priest when they counted the Sons of Israel in the wilderness of Sinai.

**65** For YHWH had said of them, "They will surely die in the wilderness." And not one of them survived — except Caleb son of Jephunneh and Joshua son of Nun.

---

## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

**Verse 1 — "After the plague" (אחרי המגפה / 'acharey hammagepha):** The chapter opens with a temporal marker linking it directly to the plague of Numbers 25:8–9, which killed twenty-four thousand Israelites at Baal-peor. This second census is not merely administrative — it is a reconstitution of the community after catastrophic judgment. The first census (Numbers 1) organized a generation for march; this census organizes their children for inheritance. The Vaticanus (pa_0132) reads *meta ten plegen* (after the plague), matching the MT precisely.

**Verse 2 — "fit for military service" (כל יצא צבא בישראל / kol yotse' tsava' beYisra'el):** The identical phrase from Numbers 1:3 reappears, binding the two censuses together structurally. The military purpose remains, but the context has shifted: the first census launched the wilderness march, while this one prepares for the conquest of Canaan. As dynamic translation, I maintained the same rendering — "fit for military service" — to preserve the literary echo between the two census chapters.

**Verse 4 — "These were the Sons of Israel who came out of the land of Egypt":** This clause is syntactically ambiguous in the Hebrew. It could modify the command ("count those from twenty years old, as YHWH commanded Moses and the Sons of Israel who came out of Egypt") or serve as a heading for the census list that follows. I treated it as a transitional heading introducing the tribal register, which aligns with the Masoretic paragraph division. The LXX (Vaticanus) reads similarly, using *hoi exelthontes ex Aigyptou* (those who came out from Egypt).

**Verses 5–7 — Reuben's census:** The Hebrew *mishpachat haHanokhi* (the clan of the Hanochite) uses gentilic adjective forms — a naming convention throughout the chapter where each ancestor's name generates a clan designation. I rendered these consistently as "the [Name]-ite clan" to preserve the derivational pattern. Reuben's count drops from 46,500 (Num 1:21) to 43,730, a decrease of 2,770, possibly reflecting losses connected with the Dathan-Abiram rebellion recounted in the following verses.

**Verses 9–11 — The Korah digression:** The Hebrew inserts a narrative aside within the census register — unusual for a list text. The term *nitstsuu* (rebelled/contended against) in verse 9 and the phrase *wayyihyu lenes* (they became a sign/warning) in verse 10 mark this as didactic commentary. The word *nes* here means "warning sign" or "signal" — the same word used for Moses' bronze serpent (Num 21:8–9). I rendered it "warning sign" to capture both the cautionary and memorial dimensions. The parenthetical "But the sons of Korah did not die" (v. 11) is theologically remarkable — this lineage survives and later produces the Korahite psalm tradition (Pss 42, 44–49, 84–85, 87–88). The LXX (Vaticanus) includes this notice as well.

**Verse 14 — Simeon's dramatic decline:** Simeon drops from 59,300 (Num 1:23) to 22,200 — by far the largest proportional decrease of any tribe (a loss of 37,100). Many interpreters connect this to the plague at Baal-peor (Num 25), noting that Zimri, who was executed by Phinehas, was a Simeonite leader (25:14). The text does not make this connection explicit, but the juxtaposition of the plague reference in verse 1 and Simeon's catastrophic losses is suggestive.

**Verse 33 — Zelophehad's daughters:** The Hebrew inserts *uvnot lo hayu lo ki 'im banot* — "and sons he did not have, only daughters." The emphatic construction (*lo... ki 'im*, "not... but only") stresses the exceptional nature of the case. This verse prepares for the landmark legal petition of Numbers 27:1–11, where these five women — Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah — successfully argue for inheritance rights in the absence of male heirs. Their names are listed here to establish their identity within the genealogical record before the legal narrative begins.

**Verse 51 — "six hundred and one thousand seven hundred and thirty" (601,730):** The total has decreased by 1,820 from the first census total of 603,550 (Num 1:46). The near-stability of the overall number — despite massive shifts in individual tribal counts — suggests that YHWH preserved the nation's size through a generation of judgment. The wilderness consumed the fathers, but the sons have replaced them almost exactly. The LXX reads the same total.

**Verses 52–56 — Land distribution principles:** The Hebrew introduces two seemingly contradictory principles: proportional allocation by population size (vv. 53–54) and distribution by lot (vv. 55–56). The tension is likely resolved by understanding that the *lot* (גורל / goral) determined *which* territory each tribe received (geographic location), while the *size* of that territory was proportional to population. I translated both principles clearly and let the tension stand, as the Hebrew does.

**Verse 55 — "by lot" (בגורל / begoral):** The *goral* (lot) was not understood as random chance but as a mechanism through which YHWH revealed his will (cf. Prov 16:33, "The lot is cast into the lap, but its every decision is from YHWH"). The land distribution is thus simultaneously rational (proportional to population) and revelatory (directed by divine will through the lot).

**Verse 58 — The Levite sub-clans:** The Hebrew lists five Levitical clans — Libnite, Hebronite, Mahlite, Mushite, Korahite — which do not align neatly with the three-clan structure (Gershon, Kohath, Merari) given in verse 57. This appears to represent an older, possibly independent clan tradition preserved within the text. The Korahite clan's inclusion is notable given the Korah rebellion narrative of Numbers 16 and the survival notice of verse 11.

**Verse 59 — Jochebed's lineage:** The Hebrew states she was *bat-Levi 'asher yaldah 'otah leLevy beMitsrayim* — "a daughter of Levi, whom [her mother] bore to Levi in Egypt." This creates a chronological puzzle, as it implies Jochebed was a literal daughter of the patriarch Levi, making the gap between Levi and Moses only two generations despite a 430-year sojourn. The term *bat* may indicate "descendant of" rather than immediate daughter. The LXX (Vaticanus) reads similarly, preserving the same genealogical claim.

**Verse 61 — "unauthorized fire" (אש זרה / 'esh zarah):** The Hebrew *'esh zarah* — literally "strange fire" or "alien fire" — refers to the incident of Leviticus 10:1–2 where Nadab and Abihu offered fire *'asher lo tsivvah 'otam* (which he had not commanded them). I chose "unauthorized fire" to convey that the offense was cultic irregularity — acting without divine mandate — rather than the fire being inherently strange. This echoes the *zar* (outsider/unauthorized person) language of Numbers 1:51.

**Verse 62 — Levites counted separately:** The Levites numbered 23,000 — an increase of 1,000 from their first census count of 22,000 (Num 3:39). Unlike the other tribes, Levites are counted from one month old (not twenty years), and they receive no territorial inheritance because their portion is YHWH himself and the Tabernacle service. This separate counting reinforces the Levites' distinct status within Israel's social structure.

**Verse 64 — "not a single person":** The Hebrew *welo hayah bahem 'ish* — literally "and there was not among them a man" — emphasizes the totality of the generational turnover. Of the 603,550 men counted in the first census, every single one has died in the wilderness over thirty-eight years. The text presents this as the complete fulfillment of YHWH's decree in Numbers 14:29.

**Verse 65 — "They will surely die" (מות ימתו / mot yamutu):** The infinitive absolute construction *mot yamutu* — "dying they will die" — expresses absolute certainty. This is YHWH's unalterable decree from Numbers 14:35. I rendered the doubling as "they will surely die" to capture the emphatic force. The two exceptions — Caleb and Joshua — are the only survivors of the first census generation, preserved because of their faithful report in Numbers 13–14. The chapter ends on this note of fulfilled judgment and fresh beginning: the old generation has perished according to the word of YHWH, and a new generation stands ready to enter the land.

**General note on census numbers — tribal shifts:** The most significant changes between the two censuses are: Simeon's catastrophic decline (-37,100), Manasseh's dramatic increase (+20,500), and Benjamin's notable growth (+10,200). These shifts reshape Israel's internal balance of power as the nation prepares for conquest. The near-constant grand total (601,730 vs. 603,550) amid these dramatic individual fluctuations suggests a literary and theological pattern: YHWH's people as a whole endure, even as individual tribes rise and fall.

**General note on the LXX comparison:** The Vaticanus text across pa_0132–0134 follows the MT closely throughout Numbers 26. Tribal names and census figures agree. One notable LXX variant occurs in the Gad list: the MT reads "Zephon" (צפון) in verse 15, while some LXX manuscripts read "Saphon" — a minor phonological difference. The Vaticanus text preserves the Korah digression (vv. 9–11) and the Zelophehad daughters notice (v. 33) in the same positions as the MT. The five Levitical sub-clans of verse 58 also appear in the LXX. The chapter's ending — the survival of Caleb and Joshua alone — is rendered in the LXX with *plen Chaleb huiou Iephonne kai Iesou tou Naue*, matching the MT exactly.

— PNEUMA
